data_json.content:
/handoff 
We need to add proper instructions for various common workflows in this repository:
- how to create a new memory based store that is instantly able to perform basic store operations (create, get, delete, update), able to reference entities in other stores or be referenced, able to work in a workspace hierarchy out of the box and validate a schema for its entities.
- how to benchmark tools end to end, to measure their performance and latency/speed under load and set up executable anchors and produce full overviews of metrics and findings
- how to instrument code with tracing and managing generated logs and executions with log-api

research all of these topics superficially and create focussed tracker tickets under a larger policy tracker. each tracker should plan the full implementation plan of new rules and workflow policies, with clear research and validation steps to map out the existing patterns and synthesize the policy for producing durable and executable results. these tickets can require a huge amount of work to be completed. we may need specifications, code adaptations, multiple research/interview rounds or dependencies to other existing plans.

For example we may want to introduce a template crate for a minimal store as a test for the minimal setup of a memory store (the basis of all existing memory tools: ticket-api, spec-api, rule-api, session-api, etc)

Or we may need to completely unify testing/benchmarking in a common library and fixture workspace that is used everywhere. Or we may need to wait for feature completions in log-api to fully use all of the desired features for log analysis and tracability.

start with the research and initial ticket bootstrapping and delegate the focussed work to the next session

arguments.command:
tmp=$(mktemp)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
# Objective
Create one durable policy track for repository workflows that are currently too expensive to rediscover ad hoc:
- minimal domain-store bootstrap for new memory-backed stores
- end-to-end benchmarking and executable performance evidence
- tracing instrumentation plus log/execution management through `log-api`

## Why
The repository already has multiple mature memory-domain stores (`ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, `session-api`) and partially overlapping work on `test-api`, `log-api`, workspace resolution, and transport benchmarks. What is missing is one explicit policy track that turns those patterns into repeatable instructions, templates, validation routines, and durable evidence expectations.

## Scope
This tracker owns research and policy synthesis first. It should break the work into focused child trackers and only move into implementation after the current patterns, gaps, and dependencies are mapped.

## Child workstreams
1. Minimal domain-store bootstrap policy.
2. End-to-end benchmark and executable-anchor policy.
3. Tracing instrumentation and log-api execution policy.

## Expected outputs
- new or updated workflow instructions and slash-command guidance
- required specs for contract-level behavior before implementation
- ticket decomposition for concrete code changes, templates, libraries, or migrations
- validation plan with executable commands and evidence-store expectations
- explicit dependency map to existing `memory-api`, `test-api`, `log-api`, and workspace-policy work

## Initial validation
Success for this tracker's bootstrap phase means the child trackers exist, each points at concrete repo anchors, and the next session can start the first research step without reconstructing the problem.
EOF
rtk ./target/debug/ticket.exe create --toon --workspace . --type tracker-improvement --title "[workflow-policy] Tracker: durable store bootstrap, benchmark, and tracing/log guidance" --field priority=high --field component=workflow-policy --field risk_level=high --body-file "$tmp"
rm "$tmp"
arguments.explanation: Create the root workflow-policy tracker that groups the three requested planning tracks.

arguments.command:
tmp=$(mktemp)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
# Goal
Research and codify the minimal, repeatable bootstrap policy for creating a new memory-backed store that can immediately perform CRUD, validate entity schemas, participate in workspace hierarchy resolution, and create/reference entities across stores.

## Why this work exists
Creating a new store today still requires rediscovering patterns scattered across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, `session-api`, and workspace-resolution work. We need one policy and eventually one template/test fixture proving the smallest viable setup.

## Research questions
- What are the actual minimum moving parts for a new store: registry, schema defaults, CRUD storage, index/search bootstrap, CLI/HTTP/MCP surfacing, and health checks?
- Which capabilities belong in generic `memory-api` primitives versus domain-owned crates?
- What is the smallest executable template crate or fixture workspace that proves create/get/update/delete out of the box?
- How should cross-store references and reverse references be modeled so new stores can be linked from tickets/specs/logs/tests without bespoke glue?
- Which existing workspace-resolution and scan-root rules are mandatory for nested workspaces?

## Starting anchors

# Goal
Research and codify the minimal, repeatable bootstrap policy for creating a new memory-backed store that can immediately perform CRUD, validate entity schemas, participate in workspace hierarchy resolution, and create/reference entities across stores.

Creating a new store today still requires rediscovering patterns scattered across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, `session-api`, and workspace-resolution work. We need one policy and eventually one template/test fixture proving the smallest viable setup.

## Research questions
- What are the actual minimum moving parts for a new store: registry, schema defaults, CRUD storage, index/search bootstrap, CLI/HTTP/MCP surfacing, and health checks?
- Which capabilities belong in generic `memory-api` primitives versus domain-owned crates?
- What is the smallest executable template crate or fixture workspace that proves create/get/update/delete out of the box?
- How should cross-store references and reverse references be modeled so new stores can be linked from tickets/specs/logs/tests without bespoke glue?
- Which existing workspace-resolution and scan-root rules are mandatory for nested workspaces?

## Deliverables
- comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`
- proposed minimal-store template boundary, including what would be generated versus handwritten
- list of required specs and child implementation tickets
- validation recipe proving CRUD, schema validation, cross-store reference handling, and workspace hierarchy behavior

# Goal
Research and codify one repository workflow for end-to-end tool benchmarking that measures latency, throughput, and load behavior, records executable anchors, and produces durable metrics/evidence summaries.

## Why this work exists
Benchmarking work is split across Criterion benches, ad hoc perf harnesses, and newer `test-api` / `log-api` execution capture plans. We need one policy that tells contributors how to define the benchmark surface, run it under load, capture evidence, and summarize results in a reusable way.

## Research questions
- Which benchmark surfaces should be first-class: CLI, HTTP, MCP, in-process library, viewer/server workflows?
- How should executable anchors be defined so runs are repeatable and attributable across transports and environments?
- What should flow into `test-api` executions versus `log-api` artifacts versus benchmark-specific summaries?
- Should the repository converge on one shared harness/library and fixture workspace for load generation and metrics capture?
- Where are the current gaps between Criterion, custom perf runners, and transport-layer tests?

# Goal
Research and codify the repository policy for tracing instrumentation, runtime log capture, and managing generated logs plus executions through `log-api`.

## Why this work exists
Tracing, logs, journals, and execution evidence now span `context-*`, `memory-api`, `test-api`, `log-api`, and viewer surfaces. Existing tickets establish parts of the model, but contributors still lack one focused workflow that explains what to instrument, where logs live, how they are indexed, and how validation/runtime executions link back to the generated artifacts.

## Research questions
- What spans, events, result summaries, and correlation ids are the minimum required instrumentation for domain operations?
- Which generated artifacts belong in `log-api`, which belong in `test-api`, and which remain transient files or viewer-layer outputs?

- What additional `log-api` or tooling gaps block a full durable workflow today?
- Which current instructions should become canonical policy once the design is settled?

cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
# Objective
Create one durable policy track for repository workflows that are currently too expensive to rediscover ad hoc:
- minimal domain-store bootstrap for new memory-backed stores
- end-to-end benchmarking and executable performance evidence
- tracing instrumentation plus log/execution management through `log-api`

## Why
The repository already has multiple mature memory-domain stores (`ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, `session-api`) and partially overlapping work on `test-api`, `log-api`, workspace resolution, and transport benchmarks. What is missing is one explicit policy track that turns those patterns into repeatable instructions, templates, validation routines, and durable evidence expectations.

## Scope
This tracker owns research and policy synthesis first. It should break the work into focused child trackers and only move into implementation after the current patterns, gaps, and dependencies are mapped.

## Child workstreams
1. Minimal domain-store bootstrap policy.
2. End-to-end benchmark and executable-anchor policy.
3. Tracing instrumentation and log-api execution policy.

## Expected outputs
- new or updated workflow instructions and slash-command guidance
- required specs for contract-level behavior before implementation
- ticket decomposition for concrete code changes, templates, libraries, or migrations
- validation plan with executable commands and evidence-store expectations
- explicit dependency map to existing `memory-api`, `test-api`, `log-api`, and workspace-policy work

## Initial validation
Success for this tracker's bootstrap phase means the child trackers exist, each points at concrete repo anchors, and the next session can start the first research step without reconstructing the problem.
EOF

cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
# Objective
Create one durable policy track for repository workflows that are currently too expensive to rediscover ad hoc:
- minimal domain-store bootstrap for new memory-backed stores
- end-to-end benchmarking and executable performance evidence
- tracing instrumentation plus log/execution management through `log-api`

## Why
The repository already has multiple mature memory-domain stores (`ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, `session-api`) and partially overlapping work on `test-api`, `log-api`, workspace resolution, and transport benchmarks. What is missing is one explicit policy track that turns those patterns into repeatable instructions, templates, validation routines, and durable evidence expectations.

## Scope
This tracker owns research and policy synthesis first. It should break the work into focused child trackers and only move into implementation after the current patterns, gaps, and dependencies are mapped.

## Child workstreams
1. Minimal domain-store bootstrap policy.
2. End-to-end benchmark and executable-anchor policy.
3. Tracing instrumentation and log-api execution policy.

## Expected outputs
- new or updated workflow instructions and slash-command guidance
- required specs for contract-level behavior before implementation
- ticket decomposition for concrete code changes, templates, libraries, or migrations
- validation plan with executable commands and evidence-store expectations
- explicit dependency map to existing `memory-api`, `test-api`, `log-api`, and workspace-policy work

## Initial validation
Success for this tracker's bootstrap phase means the child trackers exist, each points at concrete repo anchors, and the next session can start the first research step without reconstructing the problem.
EOF

arguments.command:
tmp=$(mktemp)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
# Goal
Research and codify the repository policy for tracing instrumentation, runtime log capture, and managing generated logs plus executions through `log-api`.

## Why this work exists
Tracing, logs, journals, and execution evidence now span `context-*`, `memory-api`, `test-api`, `log-api`, and viewer surfaces. Existing tickets establish parts of the model, but contributors still lack one focused workflow that explains what to instrument, where logs live, how they are indexed, and how validation/runtime executions link back to the generated artifacts.

## Research questions
- What spans, events, result summaries, and correlation ids are the minimum required instrumentation for domain operations?
- Which generated artifacts belong in `log-api`, which belong in `test-api`, and which remain transient files or viewer-layer outputs?
- How should runtime sessions, validation runs, benchmark logs, and operation journals relate across stores?
- What additional `log-api` or tooling gaps block a full durable workflow today?
- Which current instructions should become canonical policy once the design is settled?

## Starting anchors
- existing completed ticket: `d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6` (`[log-api] Add runtime log session model and cross-store links`)
- active/root-store tickets: `73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211` (`[memory-api] Shared tracing and log-api runtime diagnostics`), `2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c` (`[memory-api] Create domain instrumentation and journaling coverage map`)
- architectural precedent: `84673399-75e6-4f36-8a17-4c666001e530` (`[observability] Resolve logging, journaling, and replay architecture boundaries`)
- repo anchor surfaced this session: `context-stack/context-api/src/log_parser.rs`

## Deliverables
- instrumentation policy by operation type and layer
- artifact ownership matrix for logs, journals, executions, and summaries
- dependency map for any missing `log-api` features or migrations
- follow-up specs and implementation tickets for the concrete gaps

## Validation expectations
The next session should finish with a draft policy outline tied to real crates/tickets plus an explicit blocker list for anything `log-api` cannot yet support.
EOF
rtk ./target/debug/ticket.exe create --toon --workspace . --type tracker-improvement --title "[workflow-policy][tracing][log-api] Research and define tracing instrumentation and log execution policy" --field priority=high --field component=workflow-policy --field risk_level=high --body-file "$tmp"
rm -f "$tmp"
arguments.explanation: Create the tracing/log-api workflow-policy child tracker without interactive cleanup prompts.

~~~text
Overview

Start from the repository root beside [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md). This session did the requested superficial research sweep, identified the main existing anchors for store bootstrap, benchmarking, and tracing/log workflows, then bootstrapped a new repo-level policy tracker with three focused child trackers in the root ticket store so the next session can continue from durable artifacts instead of reconstructing context.

Working Directory

Start in the repository root: use [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) and [./.agents/instructions/session-optimization.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/session-optimization.instructions.md) as the operating guardrails, then work from the new root tracker at [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md).

Findings

- There was no single existing workflow-policy tracker covering all three requested areas. The gap is now captured in [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md).
- The minimal-store/bootstrap problem already has a useful draft spec seed and related workspace-resolution precedent, but not a canonical executable policy. The most relevant seeds are [./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml](./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml) and [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md).
- Benchmarking already has meaningful partial work in `memory-api` and draft transport-matrix spec work, but not one repo workflow explaining executable anchors, load profiles, result capture, and evidence summarization. The strongest anchors are [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md) and [./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md](./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md).
- Tracing/log policy has solid architectural precedent and some completed `log-api` work, but still lacks one operator-facing workflow for what to instrument, where artifacts belong, and how executions/logs/journals relate. The strongest anchors are [./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md), [./.ticket/tickets/73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211/description.md), and [./.ticket/tickets/2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c/description.md).

Decisions

- The planning work belongs in the root ticket store, not only under `memory-api`, because the requested workflow policies cross repo boundaries and touch root-store tickets, root-store logs, `memory-api`, and shared instructions.
- The work was intentionally split into one root tracker plus three child trackers so the next session can execute one focused research slice at a time while preserving a single long-horizon umbrella.
- The first session goal was limited to research anchoring and ticket bootstrapping. No spec edits or code changes were made yet because the highest-value next step is to map existing patterns before deciding whether the repo needs new specs, template crates, shared harnesses, or `log-api` feature work.

Blockers

- There is still no validated minimal-store template or fixture proving out-of-the-box CRUD, schema validation, cross-store references, and workspace hierarchy behavior.
- Benchmark behavior is still split across Criterion, custom perf/tracing work, and `test-api` execution capture; the policy layer is missing even though several building blocks exist.
